# Frettly

> Frettly is a document-based macOS application for exploring scales, modes, chords and fretboard theory across guitar, bass, ukulele, mandolin and banjo.

Frettly combines interactive fretboards, chord organisers and persistent document-based workspaces. It is designed for musicians, teachers and students who want to explore music theory, organise ideas and save reusable learning documents.

## Product

- [Frettly Home](https://frettly.app/): Product overview, features and App Store download.
- [Mac App Store](https://apps.apple.com/gb/app/frettly/id6757320106?mt=12): Download Frettly for macOS.

## Documentation

- [Getting Started](https://frettly.app/help/getting-started.html): Creating documents, choosing instruments and adding panels.
- [Supported Instruments](https://frettly.app/help/instruments.html): Guitar, bass, ukulele, mandolin and banjo support.
- [Document Window Overview](https://frettly.app/help/document-window-overview.html): Documents, navigation, play modes and panel management.
- [Fretboard Panels](https://frettly.app/help/fretboard-panels-overview.html): Working with scales, modes, chords, markings and fretboard controls.
- [Chord Organiser Panels](https://frettly.app/help/chord-organiser-overview.html): Building and organising chord collections and exploring voicings.
- [Settings](https://frettly.app/help/frettly-settings.html): Application, instrument, fretboard and chord preferences.

## Company

- [Efford Studio Limited](https://frettly.app/): Independent UK software studio developing Frettly.
